files.title: ORIENTATION PACKET
files.subtitle: For Newly Assigned Personnel
files.author: APD-14 Records Division
files.summary: Standard-issue orientation packet. Read this before requesting further access.
files.body:
## Welcome to APD-14

You are viewing the archive terminal for **APD (Anomalous Properties Database), Site 14**, an independent technology partner licensed by the SCP Foundation. This orientation packet is unrestricted and reissued to every new hire, contractor, and visiting researcher.

### How this terminal works

Each folder you can browse is called a **Directory**. Directories contain **Files** -- lore documents, personnel records, incident logs, and the occasional field simulation. You can move around in three ways, and you never have to use more than one of them:

- **Click or tap** any row to open it.
- **Arrow keys + Enter** to navigate without a mouse.
- **Type commands** at the prompt if you'd rather drive it like a real terminal -- try `help` for the full list.

### Clearance, in plain terms

Every file lists the clearance level required to view it, from **LEVEL 0 (Unrestricted)** up to **LEVEL 5 (Cosmic Top Secret)**. Your session starts at Level 0. If you open something above your level, the terminal will warn you and ask you to confirm before proceeding -- some files allow this override, some don't. Nothing here is a real security system; it's a framing device, so read the warnings for flavor rather than an actual locked door.

You can change your simulated clearance at any time from the status bar, or by typing `clearance` at the prompt.

### A note on redactions

Blocks of `█████` inside a document are intentional. They are not a rendering error, and there is nothing hidden underneath them to reveal.

---

If you're only here to read, the directories below this one are a good place to start. Recent hires should also review **APD // CORPORATE HISTORY & INCIDENT ARCHIVE** before requesting further access. If you're APD-14 staff adding new material to this archive, see the README that shipped with this project instead of this packet.

*Secure. Contain. Protect.*

files.title: SCP-APD-001
files.subtitle: The Quiet Ledger
files.author: Dr. ███████
files.summary: A leather-bound ledger that writes a single line whenever its owner is owed an apology they will never receive.
files.body:
**Item #:** APD-001

**Object Class:** Safe

### Special Containment Procedures

SCP-APD-001 is stored in a standard document safe in Site-19 Archival Wing B. It requires no climate control. Personnel assigned to SCP-APD-001 for research purposes must submit Form APD-7 and may not remove the object from the archival wing.

### Description

SCP-APD-001 is a leather-bound ledger, 14 cm x 20 cm, containing 212 pages of unlined paper. The cover bears no title. When held by a human subject for longer than ninety consecutive seconds, SCP-APD-001 will open to the next blank page and a single line of text will appear in handwriting matching the subject's own, describing an instance in which someone owed the subject an apology they did not receive.

Entries are historically accurate as far as they can be verified. Subjects have reported the experience as "unsettling but not unwelcome." No entry has ever repeated. SCP-APD-001 has not been observed to run out of blank pages despite consistent use since recovery.

### Addendum APD-001-1

SCP-APD-001 was recovered from an estate sale in ████████, acquired by a Foundation front company after a routine sweep flagged unusual buyer interest in the listing. No anomalous activity was reported at the point of sale. Current hypothesis places SCP-APD-001's origin with an unidentified minor reality-bending entity, designation pending.

### Description

This is placeholder test content used to demonstrate the terminal's hard-lock behavior (a file with `"overridable": false`). Replace this body once you write the real entry.

files.title: INCIDENT REPORT 77-Δ
files.subtitle: Minor Containment Breach, Archival Wing B
files.author: Security Officer T. █████
files.summary: A junior researcher held SCP-APD-001 for the full ninety-second interval out of curiosity. No injuries, no breach.
files.body:
**Filed by:** Security Officer T. █████

**Location:** Archival Wing B, Site-19

### Summary

At approximately 02:14, a junior researcher assigned to unrelated cataloguing duties in Archival Wing B removed SCP-APD-001 from its safe without authorization and held it for the full activation interval. The object performed as expected per its standard description. The researcher reported the entry as "accurate and mildly upsetting" and returned the object voluntarily.

### Findings

No anomalous side effects were observed. The safe's lock was found to be non-anomalous and simply left unsecured by the previous shift. Disciplinary action was limited to a formal note in the researcher's file and a mandatory refresher on Form APD-7 procedure.

### Recommendation

Install a keypad lock on the Wing B document safe. Logged as low-severity; no further action required.

files.title: FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT
files.subtitle: RYU HUNTER -- CONSIDERED ARMED AND EXTREMELY DANGEROUS
files.author: Foundation Internal Security Division
files.summary: Widely circulated fugitive bulletin. All personnel are required to review.
files.body:
All personnel are required to review this bulletin in full.

**SUBJECT:** Ryu Hunter

**FORMER POSITION:** Site Director, APD-14 (Anomalous Properties Database)

**STATUS:** At large. Whereabouts unknown since 2026-06-18.

### Charges

Ryu Hunter is wanted in connection with the following, arising from the loss of APD-14 on 2026-06-18:

- Unauthorized activation of a facility self-destruct protocol, resulting in the total loss of APD-14's central systems core.
- The deaths of three sitting Overseer Council members present at APD-14 at the time of the incident.
- Destruction of Foundation-licensed proprietary technology.

### Assessment

Hunter holds detailed working knowledge of APD's proprietary containment-monitoring systems and is believed to retain access to decentralized backups of that technology. Hunter should be considered a serious risk to any Foundation facility making use of APD-derived infrastructure, which, as of this filing, includes a majority of active Sites.

### Instructions

Do not approach. Do not attempt to engage or detain. If Hunter is sighted, secure the immediate area, restrict all APD-derived system access at that location, and contact Internal Security immediately through standard emergency channels.

This bulletin will be updated as new information becomes available.

directories.description: Administrative paperwork, low-sensitivity incident logs, and orientation material for personnel newly assigned to Site-19.

files.title: INCIDENT REPORT: LOSS OF APD-14
files.subtitle: Internal Security Division, Post-Incident Review
files.author: Internal Security Division
files.summary: Access denied at your current clearance. This entry cannot be overridden.
files.body:
### Summary

On 2026-06-18, APD-14 suffered the total loss of its central systems core following the unauthorized activation of a facility-wide self-destruct protocol. Three sitting members of the Overseer Council, present at APD-14 for a scheduled asset review, were killed during the resulting evacuation failure. The individual responsible, former APD-14 Site Director Ryu Hunter, remains at large.

### Background

The self-destruct protocol activated was not a Foundation system. It was built into APD-14's original architecture by APD itself, designed to destroy proprietary systems and data beyond recovery in the event of a hostile seizure attempt by a third party such as the Global Occult Coalition. It had never been triggered outside of testing. It was not designed with the Foundation itself as a threat model.

The "asset review" scheduled for 2026-06-18 was, per subsequently recovered internal correspondence, the first formal session of a Council-ordered transition: the dissolution of APD's exclusive license and the direct absorption of its proprietary systems into Foundation infrastructure, staff retained at the Council's discretion. Site Director Hunter was not informed of this in advance through official channels.

### Sequence of Events

Recovered logs place Hunter in the central systems core roughly forty minutes before the review was scheduled to begin. ████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████████. The self-destruct sequence began at approximately 14:02 local time.

Evacuation protocols for the wing hosting the review were not initiated by facility systems. Security footage from the two adjoining corridors was among the data destroyed. What can be confirmed: three Council members present for the review, designated O5-███, O5-████, and O5-█████, did not leave the wing before the systems core detonated. Their deaths are not attributed to the blast itself.

### Status of Ryu Hunter

Hunter left APD-14 by unknown means prior to the facility's emergency lockdown and has not been located. A Foundation-wide security bulletin has been issued at Level 1 clearance (see FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT). Hunter is believed to retain access to decentralized backups of APD's proprietary systems and should be considered both armed and technically capable of compromising APD-derived infrastructure elsewhere.

### Addendum

The exclusive license between the Foundation and APD is, as of this filing, suspended pending full review. APD-14 remains standing under Internal Security oversight. This archive is what could be recovered.

files.title: APD: FOUNDING & FOUNDATION PARTNERSHIP
files.subtitle: A Corporate History
files.author: APD Corporate Archives
files.summary: How a private engineering firm became the Foundation's most trusted outside contractor, and how APD-14 became its flagship site.
files.body:
## Origins

APD, Anomalous Properties Database, was founded in 2013 as a private systems engineering firm with no formal ties to the Foundation. Its founding engineers specialized in high-integrity data architecture: systems built to hold enormous volumes of sensitive material without losing a byte, without leaking a byte, and without going down.

The Foundation found them almost by accident. Following a catastrophic archival corruption event at a mid-sized Site in 2016, three independent audits failed to recover the lost containment records. APD's proposal was the fourth. It worked in six weeks, and it worked completely.

## The Exclusive License

What began as a single recovery contract became, over the following two years, a full licensing agreement. The Foundation does not typically permit outside vendors anywhere near classified material, but the volume of anomalous data being generated Foundation-wide had begun to outpace the Foundation's own engineering divisions. APD offered a solution the Foundation could not build fast enough internally: containment-monitoring software, predictive breach-detection systems, and the database architecture underlying entire Sites' record-keeping.

In exchange for exclusivity, meaning no other outside contractor would be permitted the access APD was granted, APD accepted the Foundation's full security regime: amnestic-backed nondisclosure agreements for every employee, mandatory cognitohazard-safety certification, and unannounced compliance audits. It was, by outside-vendor standards, an extraordinarily invasive arrangement. APD's leadership signed it without hesitation.

## Growth and APD-14

APD expanded quickly. Individual facilities were designated numerically in the order they were established. APD-14, built to serve Site-19's rapidly growing technical footprint, became the company's flagship location: its largest engineering staff, its primary research campus, and eventually the seat of its executive leadership.

As APD's systems became load-bearing for entire Sites' day-to-day operations, standard Foundation Mobile Task Forces proved poorly suited to responding to failures in hardware and software most of them had never been trained on. With Foundation approval, APD raised its own limited security and technical-response teams: personnel cleared to operate inside active containment environments, but answerable to APD's own chain of command rather than the Foundation's.

By its tenth year of operation, APD held a designation prefix of its own, SCP-APD-, reserved for anomalies catalogued through APD's systems. It is a level of integration rarely extended to anyone outside the Foundation itself.

## Addendum

APD-14 suffered a catastrophic systems loss on 2026-06-18. Details of the incident, and the security bulletin concerning former Site Director Ryu Hunter, are filed separately at Level 4 and Level 1 clearance respectively. This document has not been rewritten to reflect those events. It is preserved as it was filed, for the historical record.

directories.description: Founding records, partnership history, and post-incident review materials concerning APD and the APD-14 facility.

files.title: PERSONNEL FILE: RYU HUNTER
files.subtitle: Former Site Director, APD-14
files.author: Human Resources, APD-14
files.summary: Former Site Director for APD-14. Status: fugitive. See FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT.
files.body:
**Designation:** Site Director (TERMINATED)

**Assignment:** APD-14

**Status:** FUGITIVE. See FOUNDATION SECURITY BULLETIN: FUGITIVE ALERT.

### Summary

Ryu Hunter served as Site Director of APD-14 from its founding until 2026-06-18, overseeing archival operations, containment logistics, and the day-to-day welfare of APD's technical staff. Performance reviews across that period were, without exception, strongly positive: a preference for measured protocol enforcement over reactionary discipline, and a hands-on approach to onboarding new researchers personally rather than delegating orientation to subordinates.

This record is retained for historical accuracy. It should not be read as inconsistent with the events of 2026-06-18. See INCIDENT REPORT: LOSS OF APD-14 (Level 4 clearance) for the full account.

### Notes

Maintained parallel administrative ties to several independent archival and myth-cataloguing projects outside the standard APD network. Those projects are presently under review to determine whether any share infrastructure, funding, or personnel with APD, and whether any pose a continuity risk given Hunter's fugitive status.

Whereabouts unknown. Believed to retain access to decentralized backups of APD proprietary systems.

### Notes
